rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF JENNIE ACTKINSON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. Conservatorship Issues (to be addressed at hearing): 4) Leslee David Actkinson Conservator of the Estate is requesting $3,143.14 in fees based on 0.75% of the estate. No timesheets provided. Examiner cannot determine whether the request is reasonable as there may be overlap between the requested trustee and conservator of estate fees and no conservator timesheets filed. 5) Lauren Actkinson, conservator of the person is requesting $4,550.00 in fees. 6) Christopher Cole attorney for conservator of the estate is requesting $14,796.25 in fees and $362.72 in costs. Attorney is billing what appear to be the same entries in both the trust and conservatorship: See entries in trust and conservatorship for 6/30/2022 for $190.00, 6/30/2022 for $95.00 in the conservatorship, 6/30/2020 in the trust for $71.25. In the conservatorship of the estate timesheets attorney is billing for entry 10/30/2020 for $456.25 when the ex parte was filed in the trust and entry 10/28/2022 for $546.25 in the trust appear to be billing for the same thing. In the conservatorship of the estate timesheets attorney is billing for entry 10/31/2020 for $71.25 when the ex parte was filed in the trust and billed in the trust for entry 10/30/2020 for $190.00.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE JENNIE ACTKINSON TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Hearing appearance required. Please review Local Rule 14.1 D. for information regarding remote appearances. To appear by Zoom, go to Zoom.com and click Join Meeting, and use the ID and password below. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Parties appearing should check in for the hearing using their first and last name. Trust Issues (may be addressed at hearing): 1) Trustee is requesting $18,036.36 in fees based on 0.75%. Trustee is requesting $9,605.25 in extraordinary trustee fees. No legal authority cited for a request for extraordinary trustee fees. There is no such thing as extraordinary fees as a trustee. $1,500.00 was spent on property management fees that appear should be reduced from the trustee fee request as these are trustee duties. Timesheets provided allege only $11,495.25 in fees. 2) Trustee is requesting $8,112.52 for reimbursement of expenses incurred. The Exhibit A to Decl of Trustee - Expenses Advanced alleges the total is $6,317.39. It appears $311.13 was spent on Lyft. Cab/rideshare fares are absorbed as part of fees. [LRSF 14.57]. 3) Trustee is requesting to pay fees incurred by the conservatorship from the trust or alternatively that petitioner be permitted to advance $29,588.11 so the conservatorship amy pay its obligations.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF WILLIAM WADE GREENE
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #5, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/12/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1)Please file a memorandum of points and authorities for not including a supplemental account as required by Probate Code 2620 or file the required supplemental account. The final court accounting of the guardian or conservator following the death of the ward or conservatee shall include a court accounting for the period that ended on the date of death and a separate accounting for the period subsequent to the date of death. [Probate Code 2620]. 2) Notice of the hearing on the settlement of the final account or report must be given to the personal representative of the probate estate, if one has been appointed, the trustee of any trust, wherein the conservatee was a beneficiary, as well as to all of the parties as set forth in Probate Code 2621. If there is no personal representative or trustee, or if the personal representative or trustee and the conservator are the same person, then notice must be given to all devisees named in the conservatee's will, to the heirs of the conservatee, so far as is known to the conservator, or to the trust remainder beneficiaries. [LRSF 14.47 H. 5) c.] 3) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. Verification is made by original bank or brokerage statements or original bank letters signed by a bank officer with the authority to sign, showing the vesting of the account, the date, and the amount of the balance. Photocopies are not acceptable. See CRC 7.575(b) for computer-generated printouts. [LRSF 14.34 D.] An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Deem Admissions Admitted; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: UPDATED TENTATIVE RULING Part 1 of 2 This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Kilby Stenkamp, in her capacity as a real estate agent for Vanguard Properties, Inc., and Vanguard Properties' Motion to Deem Admissions Admitted is GRANTED. A motion to deem admissions admitted must show (1) a set of requests for admission was properly served on the opposing party, (2) the time to respond has expired, and (3) no response of any kind has been served. No separate statement is required. (CRC 3.1345(b).) The moving party is not required to have met and conferred, because failure to timely respond waives all objections to the RFAs and there are no issues left to resolve with the adverse party. (CCP section 2033.280(a); St. Mary v. Sup. Ct (2014) 223 Cal. App. 4th 762, 777-778.) Unless the responding party serves substantially compliant responses prior to the hearing, if the moving party has made the required showing, the court must order the RFAs deemed admitted (CCP sections 2033.220, 2033.280(c), St. Mary v. Sup. Ct (2014) 223 Cal. App. 4th 762, 778.) The papers show when and how Porchia, and his attorney of record at the time Christiane Hipps, were served the discovery requests. He was served by US mail, at his address of record, on March 9, 2021. Hipps was also served by US mail, at her address of record. Discovery requests can be served by personal service under CCP section 1011 or by mail under CCP section 1013. (CCP section 2016.050.) The March 9, 2021 Proof of Service states that the serving party followed the requirements of CCP section 1013. The address listed in the POS matches the addresses listed for Porchia and his then-attorney of record Christiane Hipps, in the Court's ROA. Porchia and Hipps were also served electronically on March 9, 2021. Any document that may be served by mail, overnight delivery or fax may be served electronically if (1) the recipient has agreed to accept service electronically or (2) local court rules authorize or require electronic service. (CCP section 1010.6(a) and (b); CRC 2.253(b)).) This was not Code-compliant service, however. The Proofs of Service state that Respondent served the documents by electronic transmission, pursuant to the state's COVID Emergency Rules generally. Emergency Rule 12, Electronic Service, was repealed effective November 13, 2020, however, months before this discovery was served on March 9, 2021. Respondent Stenkamp has carried the burden in this motion. Porchia filed no opposition. There is no showing that Porchia served any responses prior to the hearing. Under CCP section 2033.410, "Any matter admitted in response to a request for admission is conclusively established against the party making the admission in the pending action, unless the court has permitted withdrawal or amendment of that admission under Section 2033.300." However, insofar as the subject RFAs concern Porchia's communications with other parties, the statute also states, "Notwithstanding subdivision (a), any admission made by a party under this section is binding only on that party and is made for the purpose of the pending action only. It is not an admission by that party for any other purpose, and it shall not be used in any manner against that party in any other proceeding." Part 1 of 2

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion Of Respondent Kilby Stenkamp To Compel Responses To Special Interrogatories; Request For Sanctions; Memorandum Of Points And Authorities
rulings.Rulings: UPDATED TENTATIVE RULING This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Joseph M. Quinn. Respondents Kilby Stenkamp, in her capacity as a real estate agent for Vanguard Properties, Inc., and Vanguard Properties' Motion to Compel Responses to Special Interrogatories, Set One is GRANTED. Stenkamp seeks responses from Lawrence Porchia, another respondent in petitions pending in this court. If the responding party fails to respond to propounded discovery, all that need be shown in the moving papers is that a set of interrogatories was properly served on the party, that the time to respond has expired, and that no response of any kind has been served. (See Leach v. Sup. Ct. (1980) 111 Cal.App.3d 902, 905-906.) No separate statement is required, and the moving party is not required to show a reasonable and good faith attempt to resolve the matter informally with opposing counsel. (Code of Civil Procedure, section 2030.290; Sinaiko Healthcare Consulting, Inc. v. Pacific Healthcare Consultants, (2007) 148 Cal.App.4th 411.) Stenkamp has carried the burden in this motion. Porchia filed no opposition. Respondents' request for sanctions under Code of Civil Procedure sections 2023.030 and 2023.010(d), for Porchia's misuse of the discovery process in failing to respond to the discovery requests, is GRANTED IN PART AND DENIED IN PART. Sanctions are awarded to Respondents and against Lawrence Porchia in the amount of $480. To the extent that Respondents seek sanctions in excess of $480, the request is denied. Porchia to pay to Respondents' counsel, Mr. Wilson, the sum of $480 within ten days of service of notice of entry of this order. Respondent is ordered to lodge with the clerk in Department 204 a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling, as published above, by the time set for this hearing. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the court day before the hearing. Remote appearance is strongly encouraged, in conformity with the current General Order of the Probate and Mental Health Division, available on the court's website at https://sfsuperiorcourt.org/divisions/probate. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 87580177925 and passcode 722809. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF GENETTE TRAMMELL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Return Of Estate Property
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. 1. The proof of service of the executor's amended Probate Code Section 850 petition does not show the representative capacity of the petitioner (Executor) either in the caption or in item 1 of the Notice of Hearing and notice does not appear to have been given to Donald Trammell. 2. If the "conservatee" in the pleading caption is Donald Trammell, the petition must identify the conservator, submit a certified copy of Letters appointing the conservator, and that conservator must receive notice. 3. If Donald [Trammell] assigned all of his interest in the "claims, causes and/or rights of action asserted by, or which could be asserted by, Donald in this proceeding", that assignment must be filed. 4. The proposed order is very vague and does not meet the requirements of LRSF 14.7 A.2. and 3. 5. Unfiled copies of Tracy Dearman and Fusion Real Estate Network, Inc.'s papers were not reviewed. [LRSF 2.7 B. and 14.7 B. and C.] Due to COVID-19, counsel and parties should appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall whenever possible. Please review the 11th amended general order on the court's website for information on appearing remotely. For Zoom appearance: Meeting ID is 875 8017 7925 and password is 722809. For audio-only telephone appearance via Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. PARTIES APPEARING MUST CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING USING THEIR FULL NAME.

[LRSF 2.7 B., 14.7., 14.59 E. and 14.34 D.] Courtesy copies of the petition, proof of service, bank statements, and any other papers in support of the accounting and petition, and a proposed order must be received by the assigned examiner by no later than 6/10/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10.B. (Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. [CRC 2.110(a).])

rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Final Distribution For Settlement Of First And Final Account And Report Of Administration May 16, 2020-December 21, 2021)For Allowance Of Statutory Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 07/12/2022. Examiner continuances are final, not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. 1) Please explain and file a receipt for entry 10/11/2021 Payment for Hildebrand Trust to contractor-costs advanced (reimbursed 11/29/2021) for $14,397.29. Please provide the legal authority / probate code sections in support of this entry. 2) Please explain and file receipts for entry 11/17/2022 One legal-in person file petition for probate, advance, filing fee, delivery services for $549.85 as the court fee was $450.00 and the usual filing fee is only $5.00. 3) Please explain and file receipts for entry 01/31/2022 Filing Fees Petition for final distribution for $560.00 as the court fee was $435.00 and the usual filing fee is only $5.00. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/27/2022 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE CHIN FAMILY TRUST U/D/T DATED MARCH 31, 1993, as Amended by Frist Amendment to Declaration of Tr
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Successor Trustee
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/12/22. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: All petitions involving a trust must state the names and last known addresses of all vested or contingent beneficiaries. [CRC 7.902.] Unless an exception applies, such beneficiaries must either receive or waive notice. Venue is not determined by consent of beneficiaries. [Probate Code Section 17005(a).] The proof of service shows notice given only to an attorney without more. Does he represent an interested party? Item 4b of the proof of service also shows that attorney's name and address, not the city and state in which the notice was placed for collection and mailing. Not all beneficiaries appear to have consented. Why is one of David Chin's children "Phan Le Huynh FBO Chi Sieu Huynh AKA Oscar Chin"? Courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues (a supplement to the petition and proof of service of notice to all beneficiaries/interested parties) must be received by the assigned Examiner by no later than 7/1/22. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
